title: Method for preparing arylvinylsulphones
abstract: A method for preparing arylvinylsulphones comprises reacting an aryl sulphonyl halide with a reactive solvent selected from (meth)acrylonitrile or alkyl (meth)acrylate in the presence of a Cu(I) catalyst in a first reaction mixture to form an intermediate product, wherein the Cu(I) catalyst is uncomplexed. Unreacted reactive solvent is separated from the first reaction mixture to form a second mixture. A low polarity solvent is added to the second mixture to precipitate the Cu(I) catalyst and dissolve the intermediate product to form a third reaction mixture. A base is added to the third reaction mixture, wherein the intermediate product undergoes a base-catalysed elimination of the halogen atom from the intermediate product to form an arylvinylsulphone compound preferably under cooling. The arylvinylsulphone compound, is separated from the third reaction mixture.

title: Duplex stainless steel pipe
abstract: Provided is a duplex stainless steel pipe which has both high strength and excellent corrosion resistance. A duplex stainless steel pipe according to the present disclosure contains, in mass%, 0.030% or less of C, 0.20-1.00% of Si, 0.5-7.0% of Mn, 0.040% or less of P, 0.020% or less of S, 0.100% or less of Al, 4.0-9.0% of Ni, 20.0-30.0% of Cr, 0.5-2.0% of Mo, 1.5-3.0% of Cu, 0.15-0.30% of N, 0.01-0.50% of V, 0.030-0.300% of Nb, 0.10-0.50% of Co and 0.001-0.050% of Sn. This duplex stainless steel pipe has a microstructure which comprises 35.0-65.0% by volume of ferrite, not less than 0% but less than 1.0% by volume of a Ï¿ phase, with the balance being made up of austenite. The non-solid solution Nb content is 0.008% by mass or more, the ratio of the non-solid solution Nb content to the non-solid solution Al content is 1.0 or more, and the yield strength is 655 MPa or more.

title: Lamination structure
abstract: The present invention is related to a laminate (1), comprising an adhesive layer (5) made from a one-component adhesive composition, wherein the functional groups of said one component consist of isocyanate moieties, and which composition does not contain any polyurethane, said polyurethane having a weight average molecular weight of 20000 g/mol or more. The present invention is furthermore related to laminate comprising an adhesive layer (5) made from a one-component adhesive composition that is applied with a dry coating weight of less than 1 g/m<sup>2</sup>. The present invention is furthermore related to laminate comprising an adhesive layer (5) made from a one-component adhesive composition that is curable under moisture-free conditions. The present invention is furthermore related to laminate comprising an adhesive layer (5) made from a one-component adhesive composition that has a concentration of isocyanate groups in the range from 0,5 to 10%.

title: Topical compositions containing a janus kinase inhibitor
abstract: The present invention describes a topical veterinary solution composition comprising a JAK inhibitor, preferably oclacitinib, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, for use on animals, preferably canine, for the treatment of dermatological disorders, for example, allergic dermatitis, atopic dermatitis, pruritis and/or a hot spot(s).The present invention describes a topical veterinary solution composition comprising a JAK inhibitor, preferably oclacitinib, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, for use on animals, preferably canine, for the treatment of dermatological disorders, for example, allergic dermatitis, atopic dermatitis, pruritis and/or a hot spot(s). A land preparation implement includes a rotatable drum assembly that includes a tool assembly. The tool assembly includes a tool holder and a tool. The tool includes a plurality of cleats. The tool holder includes a plurality of cleat supports. The cleats and the cleat supports cooperate to facilitate releasable coupling of the tool to the tool holder.

title: Miniaturized water treatment station
abstract: The present disclosure falls within the technical area of miniaturized systems, in a laboratory and/or experimental context, defining a water treatment station. The station of the present disclosure and the elements contained therein are miniaturized compared to large-size plants such as water treatment plants in major infrastructures. The station of the present solution allows, in an innovative manner, the analysis and treatment of chemically and biologically contaminated water and the implementation of said treatment in a miniaturised structure containing at least two tanks, wherein the second tank (B202) comprises at least one water inlet and one water outlet and the first tank (B201) comprises at least one water inlet and one water outlet, the water outlet being formed in a side section of the first tank (B201).

title: Solid oral pharmaceutical composition
abstract: The present invention relates to a solid oral pharmaceutical composition comprising ketorolac or one of the salts or derivatives thereof as active ingredient. The composition takes the form of a multi-layer pharmaceutical composition comprising at least one component comprising part of the total amount of ketorolac or one of the salts or derivatives thereof exhibiting prolonged release of the active ingredient and at least one second component comprising the remainder of the total amount of ketorolac or one of the salts or derivatives thereof exhibiting immediate release of the active ingredient. By providing a specific combination of immediate-release and prolonged-release components, it is possible to obtain a composition that provides coordinated release of the drug and improves the analgesic response through the use of ketorolac. The invention also includes the process of preparing such compositions and use thereof in preparing a medicament for pain treatment.

title: Organic waste composting facility
abstract: The present application for a patent for invention relates to a composting facility (1) aimed at offering an alternative to the conventional composting system or to disposal in landfill, while at the same time producing a stabilised organic compost (composted material (MC)) for use in agriculture, replacing some of the mineral fertiliser, and avoiding problems arising from conventional composting methods. The composting facility (1) comprises a raw material receiving tank (4) made of reinforced concrete, an overhead crane (5) that supports the bucket elevator (6) operating inside the raw material receiving tank (4), rotating helicoids (7), and a helicoid conveyor (9), which distributes the raw material (M) from the bucket elevator (6) onto the composting bed (LC). The action of the buckets (19) inside the raw material receiving tank (4) generates the homogenisation of the raw materials received.

title: Fish vaccine, use thereof and method of preparation
abstract: The present invention relates to the development of a microencapsulated vaccine containing inactivated Streptococcus agalactiae, together with the adjuvants beta glucan and mineral oil, to be administered orally to fish together with feed in pelletised form or coated with the vaccine. In addition, the invention relates to the use of this vaccine to immunise fish, more specifically Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus) to activate both innate and adaptive (humoral and cellular) intestinal mucosal immunity and protect tilapia against streptococcosis. Such a vaccine can be applied in intensive fishing production systems, with the aim of reducing fish mortality, reducing the use of antimicrobials and not causing environmental damage, being easy to administer and control, reducing fish handling, and being suitable for immunising large numbers of animals at the same time without stress and at low cost.

Such a vaccine can be applied in intensive fishing production systems, with the aim of reducing fish mortality, reducing the use of antimicrobials and not causing environmental damage, being easy to administer and control, reducing fish handling, and being suitable for immunising large numbers of animals at the same time without stress and at low cost.

title: Injectable and biocompatible composite matrices for the synthesis of biosynthetic corneas and methods for their preparation
abstract: The present patent of invention relates to injectable, biocompatible collagenous composite matrices for the synthesis of biosynthetic corneas, composed of decellularized tissues incorporated with nanoparticulate materials, specifically developed for the medical-ophthalmological field. These matrices are derived from animal tissues rich in collagen, particularly porcine tissues, and are biomechanically enhanced through the addition of nanoparticles, especially metallic ones (e.g., gold, silver, and copper), using an innovative method of preparation, comminution, lyophilization, solubilization, and hydrolysis of collagen fibers. The proposed new methodology not only enables the efficient integration of nanoparticles into the tissue, but also enhances the mechanical and biofunctional properties of the resultant matrices, providing a promising framework for corneal tissue regeneration. Furthermore, these matrices have the potential to democratize access to the treatment of conditions affecting the cornea, overcoming geographic and economic barriers that limit access to corneal transplants. This advance represents a significant step toward equality in ocular health, enabling individuals in remote regions or underserved communities to have the same access to treatment as those in urban centers with greater resources.

title: Improvement applied to a static machine element known as a discharge chamber integrated into equipment known as a hydrocyclone used in the stage of verifying ore particle size quality after the crushing stage, and process of assembly, coupling, and uncoupling thereof with respect to the hydrocyclone body
abstract: The invention relates to an improvement applied to a static machine element known as a discharge chamber integrated into equipment known as a hydrocyclone used in the stage of verifying ore particle size quality after the crushing stage, and to the process of assembly, coupling, and uncoupling thereof with respect to the hydrocyclone body, represented by an evolutionary solution in the industrial and commercial sector of particle classifier/separator equipment for ores that have undergone concentration, desliming, solids recovery, and closed-circuit grinding procedures, with particular application to equipment known as hydrocyclones (Eq), wherein in order to overcome problems of premature wear and complex maintenance of the static machine element known as a discharge chamber, the invention provides an improved structural design thereof, now consisting of three components: a lower part (10), a quick-fit male part (20), and a fixed base (30), wherein a quick coupling/uncoupling concept is introduced for the operational lower part (10), enabling verification of the integrity of the cut diameter (H), distinguished in that only the quick coupling/uncoupling of the assembly formed by lower part (10) and quick-fit male part (20) need be performed.

title: Nucleic acid molecule, polypeptide, vector, cell, in vitro method, pharmaceutical composition, use, and method for treating cancer
abstract: The present invention relates to nucleic acid molecules that encode a chimeric antigen receptor (CAR). The present invention also relates to a polypeptide of the chimeric antigen receptor (CAR). The present invention also relates to a vector comprising the nucleic acid molecules defined above. The present invention also relates to the cell that expresses the polypeptide defined above. The present invention also relates to an in vitro method for obtaining a cell. The present invention also relates to a pharmaceutical composition comprising the nucleic acid molecule, vector, polypeptides or cells as defined above and a pharmaceutically acceptable vehicle. The present invention also relates to use of the nucleic acid molecule, vector, polypeptide or composition, as defined above, for the treatment of cancer. The present invention also relates to use of the nucleic acid molecule, vector, polypeptide or composition, as defined above, for the production of a drug for the treatment of cancer. The present invention also relates to a method for treating cancer in a patient.

title: Method for obtaining graphite from cast iron for the synthesis of graphene oxide
abstract: The starting material, cast iron with at least 5.0% by weight of carbon, is cast and subjected to slow cooling, producing graphite with a minimum size of 1 mm, to be ground and dissolved by acid corrosion. The graphite oxide particles are filtered and dried, forming a solution with a dose of H 2 SO 4 and kept agitated for 10 to 20 minutes at a temperature below 10ºC, then, while still being agitated, the potassium permanganate reagent is added, forming a new solution which is left to rest for at least 3 hours before being diluted in distilled water and having the excess oxidant removed by adding H 2 O 2 , left to rest again for 20 to 30 hours and then washed, producing graphene oxide, which is diluted and exfoliated in an ultrasonic bath, in order to obtain reduced graphene oxide.

title: Systems and methods for performing an autologous adipose tissue transplant
abstract: The invention relates to a method for performing an autologous adipose tissue transplant in a patient. The method may include extracting a first amount of adipose tissue from the patient into a first syringe and extracting a second amount of adipose tissue from the patient into a second syringe. The first amount of adipose tissue can be decanted to isolate the fat from the first amount of adipose tissue. The second amount of adipose tissue can be transferred into a collection tube. A stromal vascular fraction can be isolated from the second amount of adipose tissue, the stromal vascular fraction being collected in a distal portion of the collection tube. The stromal vascular fraction can then be combined with the fat, and the fat and stromal vascular fraction can be deposited into the patient.

title: Method for recovering ultra-fine materials from iron ore processing for the production of micro-pellets
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for recovering ultra-fine materials from iron ore processing for the production of micro-pellets for use in steelmaking, comprising: collecting the ultra-fine material in the form of pulp after the desliming stage of the iron ore processing method; sending the ultra-fine material to a wet magnetic concentration stage; dewatering the ultra-fine concentrate in a filter press to obtain a pellet feed; sending the ultra-fine pellet feed to a mixing stage, adding the necessary inputs for the agglomeration and hardening stage; subjecting the ultra-fine pellet feed to an agglomeration stage forming micro-pellets; subjecting the micro-pellets produced in the previous stage to a hot hardening stage in mobile grate furnaces.

title: Method for recycling phosphate batteries
abstract: The present invention relates to a novel recycling route for batteries used in the sector of electro-electronic equipment and in the automotive sector. This invention focuses on batteries of the prismatic, cylindrical and pouch types, containing lithium iron phosphate, LFP (LiFePO4), as the active material. The method comprises the following steps: discharging the batteries, dismantling and separating battery components, grinding battery cells, precipitating fluorine and lithium, leaching with acid, separating the iron with precipitation and an ion exchange resin, precipitating aluminium, and precipitating the remaining lithium. The present invention includes an acid leaching step without the use of an oxidising agent, which can reach values close to 99% efficiency for Li and Fe, and mechanical processing without any heat treatment to concentrate the metals of interest from the battery cathodes.

title: Nanostructured emulsion based on extract of brassica oleracea var. capitata, anhydrous lanolin and castor oil, for the treatment of burns and their complications
abstract: The present invention relates to a composition based on ethanolic extract of Brassica olearacea var. capitata leaves, anhydrous lanolin and castor oil, for the treatment of burns and their complications, such as infections. Emulsions made from ethanolic extract of Brassica olearacea var. capitata leaves, anhydrous lanolin and castor oil in semi-solid formulations such as creams and ointments, with 10% and 20% w/v of Brassica extract, can be used as healing agents, accelerating closure of the skin area damaged by up to 3rd degree burns, inducing the production of protective enzymes as well as the proliferation of cells and blood vessels, modulating the production of matrix components and stimulating the formation of strong scar tissue, while having no side effects due to their lack of toxicity.

title: Topical antiseptic formulation containing polyhexamethylene guanidine hydrochloride for oral use in animals
abstract: The present patent of invention relates to a novel "topical antiseptic formulation containing polyhexamethylene guanidine hydrochloride for oral use in animals" pertaining to the technical field of veterinary medicine. More specifically, it relates to a topical formulation for oral antisepsis aimed at the prevention (prophylaxis) of oral diseases in domestic animals, including caries and periodontitis. It is also intended for antiseptic use prior to and/or during clinical and surgical procedures requiring microbiological control in the oral cavity (such as dental cleaning, dental implants, oral biopsies, tooth extractions, endodontic treatments, and the application of local anaesthetics), based on polyhexamethylene guanidine hydrochloride (PHMGH). The action of this topical formulation is based on the mechanisms of action of the component PHMGH, which exhibits high antifungal and antimicrobial activity even at low concentrations, lacks hepatotoxicity, nephrotoxicity, cytotoxicity, and genotoxicity, and provides rapid and effective action in controlling microorganisms associated with conditions affecting the oral cavity of domestic animals, including dental caries and periodontitis. The proposed formulations therefore address problems arising in the technical field related to oral antisepsis, and serve both as a method for prophylaxis and for treatment or microbiological control in dental procedures, representing an alternative to conventional drugs and/or oral rinses.

title: System for collecting, storing, treating and distributing rainwater
abstract: The present invention relates to an ecologically sustainable system capable of collecting, storing, treating and distributing rainwater, and capable of separating the first millimetre of said water out of the flow line, for application in the field of civil engineering, specifically urban or rural eco-friendly water sanitation. The system comprises an interconnected ecologically sustainable system made up of three sub-systems, namely a treatment sub-system (10), an autonomous photovoltaic sub-system (13) and a pumping sub-system (32), with independent water connections upstream of a storage tank, which is thus capable of separating the first millimetre of rainwater out of the flow line. Said three sub-systems are supplied with power by a photovoltaic module (15) via an eco-friendly electrical conduit (14) and flexible cables (16), and supplied with water by means of eco-friendly ducts and connections. Said system receives rainwater by means of an input channel (1) coupled to a vertical-horizontal water pipe (3) that is connected to a cylindrical tank (31). Said vertical-horizontal water pipe (3), which has an overflow connection (2) on the vertical portion thereof, further has, on the horizontal mid-portion thereof, three connections (4) connected to a down-horizontal pipe (5) that is coupled to an underground tank (6), which communicates via a cold water pipe (7) with an upper tank (8) located adjacent to the aforementioned cylindrical tank (31). Said upper tank (8) further communicates with a horizontal-vertical pipe (9) that is connected to the treatment sub-system (10), the output of which is coupled to a vertical-horizontal outlet pipe (11) that supplies water in accordance with potability standards by means of a consumption point tap (12).

title: System and method for separating fine metal particles from the top gas of a metallurgical furnace, and metallurgical furnace for producing pig iron and separating fine metal particles
abstract: The present invention relates to a system for separating fine metal particles from the top gas of a metallurgical furnace (20), comprising: at least one gas transport pipe (10) configured to convey the top gas out of the metallurgical furnace (20); at least one coarse particle separator (11) for separating coarse particles from the top gas; at least one first collection tank (12) coupled downstream of the at least one coarse particle separator (11); at least one fine particle separator (13) for separating fine metal particles from the top gas; and at least one second collection tank (14) coupled downstream of the at least one fine particle separator (13), the at least one second collection tank (14) being configured to receive a slurry comprising the fine metal particles separated from the top gas; wherein the at least one gas transport pipe (10) forms a downstream connection between the metallurgical furnace (20), the at least one coarse particle separator (11) and the at least one fine particle separator (13). The invention also relates to a method for separating fine metal particles from the top gas of a metallurgical furnace (20), as well as to a metallurgical furnace (20) for producing pig iron and separating fine metal particles.

title: Method for treating water contaminated with manganese and iron, and corresponding equipment
abstract: The present patent of invention relates to a method for treating effluent in which a chemical reagent is dispensed directly onto aqueous liquid effluent generated continuously or intermittently, especially effluent containing iron and manganese in its composition. The invention is applicable in the field of mining in general and in the field of effluent treatment in particular, through the metered and automatically controlled introduction of calcium peroxide or calcium peroxide in conjunction with calcium oxide directly into the effluent containing the toxic metals, using autonomous equipment and instruments or controlled dissolution systems (in the form of pellets or balls), capable of accommodating different flow rates and concentrations of iron and manganese in the effluent.

title: Method for producing porous composites of nanocellulose, natural rubber latex and zinc oxide, composite and device for ultraviolet light photosensing
abstract: The present description relates to a method for producing porous composites of nanocellulose with natural rubber latex (LBN) functionalized with zinc oxide (ZnO) for UV radiation sensing. A production route is described for producing said porous composites by means of a mechanical step of agitating and sonicating an aqueous solution of nanocellulose and natural rubber latex (LBN) with zinc oxide added in powder form. The resulting composite is coated with natural rubber latex (LBN) and has anchored ZnO. A device for UV radiation photosensing is described, comprising a device body (1), formed from said porous composite (1) and a pair of electrodes (2), one electrode (2) being provided at each of the opposite ends of the device body (1).

title: Composition for increasing the stability and viability of agriculturally important microorganisms in the coating of seeds for storage; method of preparation and use thereof
abstract: The present invention provides a composition for coating agricultural seeds with agriculturally important microorganisms. A composition according to the present invention provides stabilization and viability of agriculturally important microorganisms in the coating of stored seeds, resulting in long shelf life. The present invention further provides a method for preparing a composition for coating seeds with viable microorganisms, as well as a method for coating seeds with a composition according to the present invention. The agriculturally important microorganisms according to the present invention possess multiple biological functions, including but not limited to plant growth promotion, nutrient mobilization, and control of phytonematodes and phytopathogens.

title: Compact system for treating greywater for reuse
abstract: The present invention relates to a compact system for biologically treating greywater for reuse, comprising an assembly of elements that treat greywater, wherein said greywater, after biological treatment and subsequent filtration and disinfection, leaves said system with characteristics appropriate for reuse for non-potable purposes (flushing toilets, cleaning, washing floors and streets, irrigating plants). Specifically, the greywater enters the system, undergoes initial decantation, then passes through the central part of the tank having the biorotor assembly. After going through the biorotor, the effluent again undergoes decantation and passes, via gravity, to an accumulation tank, from which it is routed, subsequently, to filtration and disinfection. Said biorotor is a partially submerged structure that slowly spins in a tank through which the greywater flows after decantation, wherein the shaft is always above the level of the effluent. The central part of the treatment system is made up of a support structure into which plastic media blocks, made from polypropylene, are inserted which have a large specific surface area, such as for example 240 m2 of surface area for each m3 of volume of material. The large contact surface provided by the honeycomb-shaped plastic structure has the function of providing a large area of contact with the effluent that passes through the rotor. As the effluent passes along the surface of the plastic media, a "biofilm" forms, which is made up of organic matter, microorganisms and air, wherein the organic matter degrades with the aim of biologically treating the wastewater or greywater.

title: Use of an iron alloy comprising a mass quantity of a niobium species as a vehicle component material and method for adjusting the mechanical properties of a metallic material
abstract: The present invention pertains to the fields of materials engineering and nanotechnology. More specifically, the invention discloses the use of an iron alloy comprising a mass quantity of a niobium species as a vehicle component material and a method for adjusting the mechanical properties of a metallic material comprising the incorporation of a niobium species. The products comprising use of the inventive iron alloy exhibit remarkable properties, such as improved mechanical properties combined with reduced weight, resulting in an exponential performance gain. In one embodiment, the alloy used in the invention is called Steeron, because it has steel characteristics while being a cast iron.

title: Chimeric vaccine antigen with triple action against ehrlichiosis, anaplasmosis and canine babesiosis, expression vector, recombinant cell, vaccine composition, diagnostic kit, treatment method and use of chimeric protein
abstract: The present invention relates to the development of vaccine formulations obtained from a chimeric recombinant protein (rTripEAB) with epitopes of T CD8+ and T CD4+ cells and built in an optimized form for bioinformatic tools, the sequence of which has conserved regions. The invention likewise relates to composition of the vaccine formulation with the chimeric antigen together with adjuvants, as well as to the evaluation of protective efficacy against infection by Ehrlichia canis, Anaplasma platys and Babesia canis. The technology is directly applicable for protection against infectious diseases in dogs, pigs, cows, horses, and humans, directly impacting the health and well-being of individuals.

title: Method for recovering iron and lead from furnace slag from pre-processing of lead-acid battery components
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for continuous or discontinuous reuse of slag (3.1) from lead-acid batteries in a smelting furnace (1), such as a blast furnace, a cupola furnace or the like, maintaining strict control of the temperature and purity of the reused substrates with recovery of iron (5) and lead (4) from slag (3.1) from lead-acid batteries, applicable in the field of metallurgy and steelmaking, specifically in the metalworking industry for recovering iron (5), lead (4) and other metals, by means of reverse recycling, including landfill restoration, allowing a more beneficial destination for this raw material, with the advantages of protecting water and soil resources from leaching and reducing the costs associated with suppling iron (5) and lead (4) for battery recycling.

title: Synthetic mat for protecting berms
abstract: The present invention relates to a synthetic mat (10) applied to berms (CM1) formed in mining fields (CM) or the like, said synthetic mat (10) comprises a base (11) made of flexible thermoplastic material (11a), such as thermoplastic elastomers - TPE - or the like, its upper surface (11b) receives, by means of a fastening screw, a layer of polypropylene fabric (12) with tying of strands (13) of raffia tape made of polyethylene and of a vibrant colour, such as lime green or the like, forming a protective covering (CP) over the berms (CM1) of mining fields (CM) to protect against bad weather; said strands (13) of raffia tape are each approximately 40 to 60 microns thick and 4.5 mm wide.

title: Lidocaine hydrochloride nasal composition for epilepsy treatment
abstract: The present invention relates to a lidocaine hydrochloride nasal composition associated with pharmacologically acceptable components in a portion of 1 ml to 1.8 ml of a highly concentrated, stable, clear transparent solution, with concentrations of lidocaine hydrochloride varying from around 1000 mcg/0.05 ml to around 1500 mcg/0.05 ml per drop, with a pH varying between around 6.0 and around 7.0 and a viscosity varying from around 1 to 100 cps, said combination being suitable for systemic intranasal release, administered in drops or assisted devices - metered dose inhalers, mDIs, around (0.05 ml to 0.20 ml) or (0.0166 ml to 0.05 ml = 1 to 3) being the necessary minimum for a therapeutic effect. Use thereof is recommended for mammals heavier than 200 g.

title: Homeopathic composition for increasing the vital energy of human beings and respective dosage
abstract: The present invention relates to a homeopathic composition developed to treat various diseases which affect human beings such as emotional disorders such as anxiety, depression, panic disorder, insomnia, schizophrenia, autism, etc., as well as all physical causes such as hypertension, myocardial infarction with or without obstruction, all autoimmune diseases, rheumatoid arthritis, autoimmune hepatitis, thyroiditis, type 1 and type 2 diabetes, all types of cancer, stroke, as well as all types of community- and hospital-acquired infections. The composition consists of 4.95 ml of sulfur (brimstone), 4.95 ml of calcium carbonate (carbonic limestone), 4.95 ml of silicon oxide (silica), 4.95 ml of phosphorus and 4.95 ml of sodium chloride (table salt), diluted in 10% apple cider vinegar.

title: Method for obtaining ready-to-drink beverage-type food composition based on acai seeds and ready-to-drink beverage-type food composition based on acai seeds
abstract: The present invention relates to a ready-to-drink beverage-type composition, obtained by infusing the powder obtained from the acai seeds in water and adding syrups or natural extracts and sweetening agents. To obtain the powder, the acai seeds are previously processed and undergo a step of neutralising the tannins present in the seed, which can take place either by means of bed fermentation or by inactivating the ethylene by soaking the seeds in a hydroalcoholic solution, therefore without the use of chemical components, and then roasted to a medium roast, in accordance with the classification of the Brazilian Coffee Industry Association (ABIC) standard of recommended quality and best practice in the production of roasted coffee beans and roasted and ground coffee. The ready-to-drink tea available is a healthy beverage, rich in antioxidants, nutrients and fibre, representing a sustainable alternative for the use of the acai seeds.

title: Water-based thermal and photocatalytic reflective coating
abstract: The present invention relates to a thermal and photocatalytic reflective coating, applied as a film to surfaces of various materials, such as zinc, fibre cement, concrete, plaster, ceramics and aluminium in the food industry, and on installations requiring a reduction in temperature and designed for sterilisation by temperature reduction. The coating comprises distilled or deionised water, acrylic or vinyl elastomeric resin, titanium dioxide (TiO2), agalmatolite and niobium pentoxide (Nb2O5), and has the advantages of being highly adhesive to the substrate, acting over a wider range of visible light reflection, presenting high thermal and photovoltaic performance, having low treatment costs, significantly increasing photoactivity, generating active substances that keep structures waterproof and clean, greatly reducing the absorption of infrared rays, having greater durability, acting with higher quality, and having a high thermal performance, and greater durability and performance of the coating.

title: Human anti-fgf2 monoclonal antibody (mab), pharmaceutical composition comprising same, use thereof and kit for the detection of cancer in a biological sample comprising said mab
abstract: The present invention refers to a human anti-FGF2 monoclonal antibody (mAb) comprising the amino acid sequence of the light chain as defined by the SEQ ID NO: 1 or 3, and the amino acid sequence of the heavy chain as defined by the SEQ ID NO: 2 or 4. Additionally, the present invention refers to a pharmaceutical composition comprising said mAb and to the use thereof in the production of a medicament for treating cancer. Furthermore, the present invention relates to a kit for the detection of the FGF2 protein in a biological sample comprising said mAb or a biologically active fragment thereof bound to a detectable fraction, which can be used for prognosis or targeting cancer treatment. In addition to the therapeutic use associated with cancers, such as inhibition of angiogenesis, there are potential applications in the control of fibrotic diseases, neutralizing the excessive action of FGF, for example in intense tissue repair diseases (keloids), and idiopathic fibrosis or fibrosis secondary to chronic inflammatory diseases. In addition to their therapeutic potential, antibodies can be used for immunoscintigraphy, labeled with radioisotopes, for molecular imaging and prediction of fibrosing responses.

title: Functional nanoparticle premix, process for obtaining same, process for modifying material properties and material with modified properties
abstract: The present invention pertains to the field of materials engineering and nanotechnology. More specifically, the invention discloses: a functional premix comprising nanoparticles and a vehicle or compatibiliser, a process for obtaining same, a process for modifying material properties, and modified materials provided with improved properties. The premix of the invention, surprisingly, is useful for modifying the properties of materials of different kinds, such as polymers, metals, ceramic materials and/or composites of different materials. The premix of the invention provides various advantages in the transport, use and incorporation of nanoparticles into different materials. In one embodiment, the invention provides remarkable modifications to the properties of polymers and represents a practical departure from previous concepts in this technical field. In another embodiment, the invention provides advantages in the modification of the properties of metallic and/or ceramic materials.

title: Method for converting liquid plant biomass in a delayed coking unit
abstract: The present invention relates to the coprocessing of liquid plant biomass of lignocellulosic origin and residual fractions from petroleum distillation, in which the injection of the renewable charge is carried out through the top of the coker, avoiding the problems of accelerated coking in the oven tubes and obstructions in the unit lines which inevitably occur when this type of renewable charge is coprocessed in a mixture with the main (fossil) charge. In the present invention, the renewable material is injected through the top of the reactor, in conjunction with an accelerating gas, by means of a suitable injector, so as to minimize the possibility of obstruction of the system during injection. Due to the physical and chemical characteristics of lignocellulosic charges, the addition of this type of charge through the top of the reactor could also lead to difficulties related to the possibility of obstruction of the line in the reactor inlet, since the usual top temperature inside such reactors is around 440°C. In order to overcome this technical problem, the present invention proposes using an auxiliary charging system for injecting bio-oil and a gas stream, comprising a tank, a pump and a charging injector. The injection system of the present invention promotes increased speed, cooling and improved dispersion of the renewable charge in the inlet of the reactor.

title: Skin cosmetic formulation of papain, urea, zinc sulfate, and ubiquinol for treating and healing melasma
abstract: The present patent application is characterized essentially by a chemical formulation based on papain, urea, zinc sulfate and ubiquinol, the aim of which is to treat and heal skin affected by melasma, the formulation being composed of: 10% papain; 5% urea; 6% zinc sulfate and 5% ubiquinol, in a cream base q.s. 100g. This formulation must be produced by a trained professional who understands chemical processes and the use of each composition for proper handling. The product is for nighttime topical use, for any type of skin, and must be applied to clean, dry skin. The formulation should be applied to the area of the melasma and, once absorbed into the skin, sunscreen should be used on top of the product. The minimum period of treatment should be 6 to 12 months. Said formulation is considered to combat cellular senescence, repair the stratum corneum and the phospholipid bilayer of the cells, combat lipidic peroxidation and local inflammation, eliminate excess free radicals in the intracellular environment, reorganize the structure of internal organelles and preserve DNA, thereby maintaining homeostasis and skin health, healing and reducing dark spots, and leaving the skin healthier, soft, bright and moisturized.

title: Ultra-low manganese steel coil alloyed with niobium, hot-rolled for structural applications and method of manufacture thereof
abstract: The present invention relates to a steel with a predominantly ferritic microstructure, with residual percentages of pearlite and/or bainite, supplied to the market in coil form and originating from flat steel mills. The manufacturing route for ultra-low manganese steel with the addition of niobium according to the invention is via flat steel rolling with a finished end product in coil format and a method of manufacture for this hot-rolled, high-strength, ultra-low manganese steel coil with the addition of niobium, without the addition of manganese in the melt shop and with the addition of low niobium contents, taking advantage of the favourable condition of working with ultra-low manganese, low-sulfur and niobium-alloyed steels.

title: Hybrid protein, hybrid nucleotide sequence, nucleic acid molecule, vector for hybrid protein expression, use of hybrid protein or nucleotide sequence, formulation, process for producing a formulation and method for treating lesions and/or cancer caused by human papillomavirus (hpv) infection
abstract: The present invention describes an approach for improving immunogenic and therapeutic performance in the prevention and/or treatment of lesions and/or cancer caused by or associated with the human papillomavirus (HPV). More specifically, the approach involves a hybrid protein selected from two entities with improved immunogenic and therapeutic potential, use thereof and a formulation comprising same. Also disclosed are nucleotide sequences, a vector for the expression of recombinant proteins, as well as a process for producing a formulation comprising said entity and a therapeutic method. The invention applies to the fields of medicine, pharmacy and biotechnology.

title: Non-toxic hypergolic bipropellant with a catalytically promoted fuel system and a solution of hydrogen peroxide as oxidising agent, and use of same
abstract: The present invention relates to non-toxic hypergolic bipropellants with catalytically promoted fuel systems and highly concentrated hydrogen peroxide solution which ignite spontaneously when they come into contact with each other. The non-toxic hypergolic fuel systems of the present invention comprise two propagators based on polyamine and alkanolamine, promoted by reduced amounts of catalyst. The present invention further describes the use of lower alcohol compounds as stability/solubility enhancing agents. A potential bipropellant combination must meet several requirements, such as low ignition delay time (IDT < 20 ms), low viscosity, good storage stability and performance comparable to conventional hydrazine/NTO-based fuels available in the prior art. The novel hypergolic fuel systems with a solution of hydrogen peroxide described in the present application are efficient substitutes for standard methylhydrazine fuel for use in rockets and spacecraft. The present invention further relates to the use of said fuel system in space equipment or operations in pulse and/or multiple restart mode, preferably rockets and spacecraft.

title: Method of manufacturing powdered materials by controlled spraying of atoms of a target material and subsequent deposition on a powdered base material
abstract: The present invention discloses a method of manufacturing novel powdered materials by controlled spraying of atoms. In this method, the material to be deposited (target) can be chosen from among any of the elements in the periodic table or a combination of at least two of said elements. The material that will receive the deposit, called the base material or substrate, can be chosen from among the following materials: polymeric, metallic, semiconducting, ceramic, molecular sieves, perovskites, MOFs, carbonaceous materials or any type/class of powdered material with a vapour pressure of less than 10-1mBar. The method taught by the present invention describes means of planning and achieving different coating structures for the base material (substrate) by defining and controlling production processes. This makes it possible to derive a plurality of novel materials with novel properties as well as predefined properties.

title: Moisturising cream with long-lasting antiseptic protection for hands
abstract: The invention provides a dermatologically tested moisturising cream with long-lasting antiseptic protection of up to 4 hours, said protection being made more effective by eliminating the need to frequently reapply said cream to ensure protection against bacteria, disease-causing microorganisms and viruses, the cream basically having the following formula: deionised water - 100.00% q.s. (solvent); polyacrylamide, C13-14 isoparaffin and laureth 7 - 1.00 to 3.00% (emulsifier and viscosity agent); liquid paraffin - 0.50 to 3.00% (skin conditioner); alcohol - 15.00 to 20.00% (solvent); Chemynol F - 0.50 to 1.00% (preservative); PHTALOX® functional bioactive phthalocyanine - 0.05% (active), which continuously produces reactive oxygen in the presence of molecular oxygen. The inventive cream has a huge range of markets in which it can be used, for example: in the health sector in general clinics, physiotherapy clinics, veterinary clinics, dental clinics, laboratories, hospitals, health care, patients, etc.; in the food sector in fast food chains, industries, restaurants, catering, food service, gastronomic events, etc.; and in the retail sector in pharmacies, supermarkets, gyms, shopping centres, hotel chains, beauty salons, schools, colleges, public transport, private urban transport, bus stations, airports, etc. This is a solution of an evolutionary nature, the practical result of which is exceptional and effective useability.

title: Pulley for reels and hobbles
abstract: The present invention relates to the field of pulleys and sheaves in animal slaughter systems and describes pulleys for hobbles or reels which are made of a high-strength polymer and have self-lubricating properties, for use in bovine, swine, ovine and caprine slaughter lines Currently, the pulleys of such hobbles or reels are made of carbon steel or stainless steel and slide on a metal rail, generating friction between the metals and consequently generating metal shavings, which fall onto the surface of the carcasses of fresh animals, which are hung by their legs by hobbles or reels, said carcasses being contaminated at slaughter due to lubrication of the hobbles or reels, moving under the effect of gravity. Thus, use of the pulley of the present invention does not cause steel shavings because here the friction is between a polymer and the metal of the rail, and there is no need for lubrication because the material is self-lubricating.

title: Premix for preparing basalt fibre, basalt fibre, use of the basalt fibre, premix for preparing a composite, method for preparing the composite, and composite
abstract: The present invention relates to the fields of materials engineering, chemistry and mechanical engineering. More specifically, the present invention describes a basalt fibre composition, a process for preparing basalt fibre, use thereof and a composite comprising basalt fibre. In one embodiment, the invention provides a basalt fibre comprising a combination of three different rock samples. The invention also relates to a method for preparing basalt fibre by melting the rock combinations, generating a continuous fibre by forming the molten material and a composite comprising basalt fibre. Said basalt fibre is also an alternative to glass fibre and carbon fibre. Glass fibre involves known occupational risks and therefore the present invention allows the elimination of waste related to glass fibre use and production, complying with ESG (environmental, social and governance) best practices. The basalt fibre of the present invention allows performance which is unattainable by glass fibre, at a much lower cost than carbon fibre.

title: Compositions for tissue repair and hair growth containing an oligopeptide complex of the growth factor kgf-2, polyanion and nanospheres and their uses
abstract: The invention relates to compositions containing biomimetic peptides derived from the growth factor KGF-2 (Keratinocyte Growth Factor-2 or fibroblast growth factor-10 (FGF-10)) in a highly stable complex system with a biomimetic polyanion biopolymer, preferably hyaluronic acid, in a biocompatible and hypoallergenic nano-bead transport system that allows assertive, high-performance transport of said oligopeptide/polyanion complex. The invention also involves a hair tonic and the use of these compositions to modulate the atypical changes associated with ageing hair in order to reactivate the hair growth cycle by coordinating and synchronising the anagen phase, the specific growth phase of the hair follicle structure, maintaining and stimulating hair growth, as well as preventing early hair loss, in a way that is healthy/biocompatible for hair structures, specifically for the hair cell microenvironment. In addition, the compositions presented here act to delay skin ageing through tissue repair.

title: Method for treating metal surfaces, treated metal, phosphating additive, and method for phosphating metal surfaces
abstract: The present invention pertains to the fields of materials engineering and chemistry. More specifically, the invention discloses a method for treating metal surfaces, as well as treated metal for products of economic interest. The invention further relates to a phosphating additive and a method for phosphating metal surfaces comprising mixing said additive with a phosphating agent. The treatment method of the present invention has surprising results for the treatment of metal surfaces. In addition, it enables replacement of the widely used phosphating process for treating metal surfaces, also providing a process with fewer treatment stages than conventional phosphating. This results in reagent savings and reduced waste generation, aligning with ESG (environmental, social and governance) practices.

title: Multi-epitope antigen, immunogenic composition comprising said antigen, pneumococcal diagnostic kit, and uses of said antigen
abstract: The present invention relates to a multi-epitope antigen comprising 20 epitope blocks of 20 to 36 amino acids from proteins selected from the group consisting of NanA, PcsB, PhtD, Ply, PncO, StkP, PspA-F1 and PspA-F2. In addition, the present invention relates to an immunogenic composition comprising said antigen and a pharmaceutically acceptable vehicle and/or adjuvant. In addition, a pneumococcal diagnostic kit comprising said antigen or a biologically active fragment thereof bound to a detectable fraction; an antibody generated by said antigen; and instructions for use is also disclosed. In addition, the present invention relates to the use of said antigen to be used in the preparation of a vaccine to prevent diseases caused by pneumococcus bacteria; and for the production of monoclonal and polyclonal antibodies. Lastly, the present invention relates to an in vitro method for diagnosing pneumococcus which comprises applying the said antigen or a biologically active fragment of it bound to a detectable fraction, in contact with samples of body fluids selected from among blood, mucus expelled from the lower airways and urine.

title: Thermally processed polymer matrix nanocomposites incorporating graphene with or without functionalisation
abstract: The present invention patent relates to the processing of graphene in polymeric matrices, preferably thermoplastics and/or elastomers, obtained by thermal processing methods and microstructural characteristics, with their significant gains for various industrial uses. The graphene nanoplates used can contain from 1 to 30 layers (multilayer graphene - MLG) or 1 to 50 layers (nanoplate graphene - GNP) and are obtained by exfoliating commercial graphites. These quasi-two-dimensional nanomaterials have excellent mechanical properties or options for modification into nanocomposites (functionalisation). Polymer substrates and/or resins are made up of compounds with repeating small units (mers) or high molar mass macromolecules. Analyses of the mechanical performance of the nanocomposites revealed a significant increase in ductile behaviour with an increase in the modulus of elasticity and toughness and a significant reduction in polymer hydrolysis and/or tribochemical degradation after the addition of graphene to the polymeric matrices, in the polymeric matrices, in paints or in the bulk of the nanocomposites.

title: Unit for continuous production of steelmaking coal and energy using microwave-assisted pyrolysis
abstract: The present invention patent application relates to a reactor and its peripheral equipment, referred to as a unit for continuous production of steelmaking coal and energy, used in the production of charcoal, oil and non-condensable gases from the transformation of wood by means of pyrolysis assisted by non-ionising microwaves on a metal conveyor belt and in a controlled atmosphere, characterised in that it uses several low-power microwave-generating sources distributed along the cavity, characterised in that it uses an automatic control system that guarantees the safety of the charcoal production, ensuring stable physical and chemical properties. It is distinguished by its high production capacity; full utilisation of the timber, transforming it into (3) products; the absence of pollutants; a reduction in the industrial process cycle from (288) hours to (3) hours; a control system capable of estimating the physical and chemical parameters of the coal, and a safety and self-diagnosis system.

title: Cold-pressed solid agglomerate for the production of iron-nickel alloys, manufacturing process of the same, and, production process of an iron-nickel alloy
abstract: The present invention relates to a cold-pressed solid agglomerate for producing iron-nickel alloys, the manufacturing process thereof, and a process for producing iron-nickel alloys. The present invention features a cold-pressed solid agglomerate with the following mass proportions: 80 to 95% by mass of laterite ore; 3 to 10% by mass of at least one binder; and 2 to 10% by mass of a carbon source. The agglomerate manufacturing process comprises the steps of (i) mixing the laterite ore with at least one binder and a carbon source; and (ii) cold pressing the mixture to form the agglomerate. The process of producing an iron-nickel alloy comprises the steps of (i) mixing the laterite ore with at least one binder and a carbon source; (ii) cold pressing the mixture to form an agglomerate; (iii) pre-reducing the solid agglomerate in a furnace with a controlled atmosphere; and (iv) melting the pre-reduced solid agglomerate in a furnace with a controlled atmosphere.

title: Composition of anti-corrosion coating
abstract: The present invention pertains to the field of coating compositions and, more specifically, it involves a multi-layered anti-corrosion coating (R) composition comprising at least three layers: the base layer (1) an electrolytic or organometallic base layer, a nanoceramic sealant as the intermediate layer (2), and an outer layer (3) of epoxy or polyester-polyurethane resin. The base layer (1) consists of an organometallic dispersion based on zinc and aluminium alloys or a zinc or zinc alloy base applied electrolytically to a metallic surface (not illustrated). Thus, in addition to being cost-effective, the coating (R) of the present invention demonstrates significantly higher efficiency against acid-induced corrosion than coatings in the state of the art, offering resistance to at least 40 cycles of Kesternich AHT 2.0 S testing.

title: Separation process for suspension left over after fermentation of organic waste to form biogas, comprises mild dewatering and vibrating membrane filtration steps
abstract: The suspension is pre-treated by mild dewatering and then subjected to vibrating membrane filtration. A process for separating an aqueous liquid suspension of organic material into a concentrated solid fraction and a liquid fraction with a low solids content comprises an optional coagulation and flocculation step, a preliminary mechanical separation step comprising at least one so-called mild dewatering stage, and a subsequent membrane filtration step comprising vibrating membrane filtration. Independent claims are also included for the following: (1) Apparatus for carrying out this process; (2) Energy-efficient integrated treatment method for manure and/or at least one organic waste stream, comprising bio-fermentation of the waste and recovering the biogas; burning the biogas in a motor to produce electricity and heat; separating the sludge from the bio-fermentation step into a wet solid fraction and a liquid fraction with a low solids content using a preliminary mechanical separation step comprising at least one mild dewatering stage, followed by vibrating membrane filtration; and drying the solid fraction using the heat generated during burning of the biogas; and (3) Installation for performing this treatment method.

title: Latex formulation used as backing for artificial turf, comprises vinyl substituted aromatic polymer, conjugated diene, unsaturated carboxylic acid and cross linkable monomer
abstract: The latex comprises an aqueous emulsion polymerization product of a vinyl substituted aromatic polymer, a conjugated diene monomer, an ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid monomer or alpha ,beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid monomer, and an ethylenically unsaturated monomer having a functional group capable of cross-linking. A synthetic turf material has a cross-linkable latex formulation coated on one side as a backing, the formulation comprising (A1) an aqueous emulsion polymerization product of (a) 30-79.9 wt.% vinyl-substituted aromatic polymer, (b) 20-69.9 wt.% conjugated diene monomer, (c) 0.1-20 wt.% at least one ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid monomer or an alpha ,beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid monomer (e.g. acrylic acid or itaconic acid), (d) 0.1-20 wt.% one or more monomers having at least one ethylenic bond and one or more functional groups capable of undergoing cross-linking reactions, e.g. hydroxyl, carboxyl, amine (mono or disubstituted), amide (mono or disubstituted), (alkyl)sulfonyl, aldehyde, anhydride, alkoxy, cyano, nitro, acetoacetoxy, isocyanate, acetoxyl, hydrolizable silane or epoxy groups, and (e) 0-20 wt.% monomer having non-functional groups incapable of undergoing cross-linking reactions (e.g. (m)ethyl methacrylate) and which can be polymerized with at least one of monomers (a)-(d), with respect to the sum of the weight of monomers (a)-(e), and (A2) 0-20 (0.2-10) wt.% cross-linking agent (mixture), with respect to the weight of the copolymer. Independent claims are also included for the following: (A) Cross-linkable latex formulation as described above; and (B) Method for preparing the synthetic turf material by applying the formulation to the material and curing the coating at 30-200[deg] C, preferably from 50[deg] C to less than 100[deg] C.

title: Visual communication panel for road signs, contains light emitting enamel layer comprising pigment and transparent enamel
abstract: The light emitting enamel layer (5) comprises a transparent enamel and a light emitting pigment. A visual communication panel (1) comprises a substrate (2-4) made from enamelled steel, on top of which is a light emitting enamel layer comprising a transparent enamel and 1-70 wt.% light emitting pigment, which in turn is covered by an enamel top layer (6). An independent claim is also included for a method for making the panel, comprising the following steps: (a) mixing 100 parts by weight transparent enamel powder with 25-75 parts by weight liquid screen printing medium; (b) mixing 60 parts by weight light emitting pigment with 15-25 parts by weight liquid screen printing medium; (c) mixing 150 parts by weight of the mixture from step (a) with 60-100 parts by weight of the mixture from step (b); (d) applying the resulting paste to a substrate optionally provided with a base layer and/or colored layer, using a coarse screen (10-77 T) to form a layer with a thickness of 10-400 microns; (e) firing the applied pigmented enamel layer for 1.5-3 minutes at 600-950[deg] C; (f) repeating step (a); (g) applying the mixture from step (f) onto a light emitting enamel layer provided on top of a substrate, using a fine screen (50-150 T) to form a layer with a thickness of 5-20 microns; and (h) repeating step (e).

title: Cultivation method for Agastache foeniculum, used to prepare essential oil for food, drink and pharmaceutical products, uses sandy clay soil and climate with specific temperature and humidity
abstract: The plants are cultivated in a soil with specific clay and sand contents, in a climate with a specific average temperature and relative humidity. A method for growing Agastache foeniculum involves cultivation in a clay-containing sandy soil comprising 50-90% clay and 10-50% sand, in a climate with an average temperature of 10-30 (15-24)[deg] C during at least two months per year, and a relative humidity of 35-90 (43-82)%. Independent claims are also included for the following: (i) Preparation of an essential oil from Agastache foeniculum grown by this method, by hydrodistillation of the crude plant material; (ii) Essential oil prepared by this method; (iii) Hydrolate obtained from this hydrodistillation process; (iv) Syrup of Agastache foeniculum, comprising parts of the plant such as the flower, leaves and/or stem in the form of an infusion, a macerate or a decoct, added to a sugar-comprising substance; (v) Preparation of a honey using Agastache foeniculum, by seeding the plant at different intervals, preferably with a first batch in April, a second batch in May etc., so that plants are in bloom throughout the summer, the number of plants per beehive being at least 12000 (20000); (vi) Alcoholic extract of Agastache foeniculum grown by this method; and (vii) Plant extract of Agastache foeniculum grown by this method.

title: Floor panel for forming a floating floor covering in humid rooms e.g. bathrooms, comprises single- or multi-part substrate with water- or moisture-absorbing material, top layer, and profiled edge regions at its two opposite sides
abstract: The floor panel for forming a floating floor covering in humid rooms such as bathrooms, comprises a single- or multi-part substrate (15) with a water- or moisture-absorbing material, a top layer (16), and profiled edge regions having coupling parts. The profiled edge regions are placed at two opposite sides of the panel. A portion formed of the water- or moisture-absorbing material has an active agent (23) with moisture-repellent effect, a hardened monomer-free water-based UV lacquer, and a solidified polyurethane hot melt glue. The active agent is present on all edge regions of the panel. The floor panel for forming a floating floor covering in humid rooms such as bathrooms, comprises a single- or multi-part substrate (15) with a water- or moisture-absorbing material, a top layer (16), and profiled edge regions having coupling parts. The profiled edge regions are placed at two opposite sides of the panel. A portion formed of the water- or moisture-absorbing material has an active agent (23) with moisture-repellent effect, a hardened monomer-free water-based UV lacquer, and a solidified polyurethane hot melt glue. The active agent is present on all edge regions of the panel, is a solid product obtained from a mixture or dispersion having water, or is a residue obtained by vaporization or drying of the mixture at a surface of the profiled edge regions. A complete sealing against water and moisture is obtained at the profiled edge region surface. The profiled edge region surface is made as a chamfer or bevel provided with a decorative and/or transparent coating formed using the hardened monomer-free UV lacquer. The hardened monomer-free water-based UV lacquer and the solidified hot melt glue are provided on a surface of the coupling parts to form a moisture-repellent coating. The solidified hot melt glue is present over a part of the length of respective profiled edge regions as a strip underneath the upper edge of the floor panel. An independent claim is included for a method for manufacturing a floor panel.

title: Filter for composite water and sewage treatment, has thermoplastic polymer filters having coarser diameter for its irregular ringed filaments and larger space for its interspaces than one positioned behind
abstract: The filter device (10) comprises extruded thermoplastic polymer filters (101-103) which form irregular ringed filaments that are mutually twisted, entangled together and piled up, with interspaces in between to let sewage flow through. Each filter has a coarser diameter for its ringed filaments and a larger space for its interspaces than the one positioned behind. A filter device for composite water treatment and sewage treatment comprises high molecular weight thermoplastic polymer filters manufactured via thermal extrusion to form irregular ringed filaments that are mutually twisted and entangled together and then piled up. Interspaces are formed between the ringed filaments to let sewage flow through. Each filter has a coarser diameter for its ringed filaments and a larger space for its interspaces than the one positioned behind.

title: Water purification installation for household effluent, includes air lift system with control device for generating air pulses with high pressure and flow rate compared with lift pressure
abstract: The air lift system for supplying water from the pre-sedimentation compartment (4) to the aeration compartment (5) includes a control device for generating air pulses with a higher pressure and flow rate compared with the lift pressure. A water purification installation comprises a small tank (1) with a volume less than 100 m3> and containing at least one internal wall (2, 3) dividing the tank to form a pre-sedimentation compartment and an aeration compartment. The tank includes an inlet (7) for supplying effluent to the pre-sedimentation tank, a means for transporting water from the pre-sedimentation compartment to the aeration compartment, an outlet (9) for discharging water from the aeration compartment, an air or gas lift system for supplying water from the pre-sedimentation compartment to the aeration compartment, and a supply of pressurized air or gas for the lift system. The lift system includes a control device with at least one means for supplying pulses of air or gas to the system over one or more periods of less than 1 (0.25) minute, the pulse pressure being at least 25 (50) % higher than the average air or gas lift pressure generated over one or more periods of more than 10 minutes and/or the pulse flow rate being at least 25 (50) % higher than the average air or gas lift flow generated over one or more periods of more than 10 minutes. An independent claim is also included for a method for purifying effluent from one or more houses, comprising at least partly purifying the effluent using the above installation.

The lift system includes a control device with at least one means for supplying pulses of air or gas to the system over one or more periods of less than 1 (0.25) minute, the pulse pressure being at least 25 (50) % higher than the average air or gas lift pressure generated over one or more periods of more than 10 minutes and/or the pulse flow rate being at least 25 (50) % higher than the average air or gas lift flow generated over one or more periods of more than 10 minutes. An independent claim is also included for a method for purifying effluent from one or more houses, comprising at least partly purifying the effluent using the above installation.

title: Cosmetic comprising hop and red clover, used for preventing skin aging and accelerating the growth of and strengthening hair and nails
abstract: A composition comprising hop and red clover is used as a cosmetic. A composition comprising hop and red clover is used as a cosmetic, especially for preventing skin aging and for accelerating the growth of and/or strengthening hair and nails. Independent claims are also included for the following: (A) Use of the composition for preparing a cosmetic product; (B) Tablet or capsule comprising hop, red clover, buckwheat, a zinc salt, rye and optional fillers; and (C) Method for preparing a solution or suspension for preventing skin aging or for accelerating the growth of and/or strengthening hair and nails, by mixing one of these tablets or capsules with water.

title: Water sterilization device powered by solar energy, includes bypass circuit for sterilizing heat exchanger
abstract: A bypass valve (13) is provided for diverting the liquid to a circuit (A) used to sterilize the heat exchanger (5) normally used to cool the sterilized liquid. A device for sterilizing a liquid uses solar energy to heat the liquid to a suitable sterilization temperature without it boiling, the latter being achieved by heating the liquid under pressure and placing an overpressure valve (9) at the liquid outlet. The liquid circulates around a conduit in the center of a double-walled glass tube which is heated by solar energy. Liquid is introduced via a pump (3) which is driven by an electric motor (11) regulated via a temperature sensor (7, 14) which switches the pump off when the temperature of the liquid falls below the sterilization temperature. The pump is powered by a solar panel (12). The sterilized liquid is delivered to a thermally insulated vessel (8) located in the liquid circuit and then cooled using a heat exchanger which is heated according to a counter-flow principle, the heat transferred being used to sterilize untreated liquid. A bypass valve (13) is provided which when opened diverts the! flow of water to the heat exchanger to a circuit (A) behind the latter which is used to heat the heat exchanger to a sterilization temperature without heat being transferred to the untreated liquid.

title: METHOD FOR THE TREATMENT OF WALLS against rising damp AND DEVICE USED THEREBY.
abstract: A first liquid and a second liquid comprising a thickener are mixed together immediately before, during or after injection into the wall (1) in order to form an impermeable layer for the subsequently injected water-repellent products. A method for treating walls to protect against rising damp comprises injecting one or more liquid, water-repellent products into the wall at a second level (B) after having first of all formed a layer impermeable to these products at a first level (A) below the second level by injecting a first liquid and a second liquid comprising a thickener for the first liquid. These two liquids are mixed together immediately before, during or after injection into the wall. An independent claim is also included for the apparatus used to carry out this method, comprising a two or multi-component dosing or injection installation with at least two separate reservoirs (12, 13) and an outlet (7) with two or more separate channels (14, 15), each of which is connected to one of the reservoirs and has its free end in communication with a common outlet nozzle (16).

title: Processing blood from slaughterhouse waste to form product useful in e.g. food or animal feed, comprises coagulation, hydrolysis with base and addition of acid to form precipitate
abstract: The blood is heated to coagulate it, the coagulate is hydrolyzed using a base and an acid is added to the hydrolyzate to form a precipitate. A method for processing animal waste blood comprises the following steps: (A) heating the waste to a given temperature in order to obtain a coagulate and a liquid phase and then removing the liquid phase; (B) hydrolysis of the coagulate in an aqueous medium by adding a basic solution; and (C) adding an acidic solution to the blood hydrolyzate in order to form a precipitate comprising the processed blood. Independent claims are also included for (a) the process apparatus comprising a coagulation and hydrolysis tank (12) and (b) processed blood produced by this method.

title: Gas injection device for bulk material in e.g. reactor bed or silo, comprises distributor with gas supply, distribution box, central box support and blow holes
abstract: Gas is injected via a distributor (b) located inside a casing containing a bulk material (a) and comprising a central gas supply (d), a gas distribution box, an expandable central support (3) for the box and a number of blow holes. A device for injecting a gas into a granular material comprises an internal gas distributor mounted inside a casing containing a bulk material into which a gas (mixture) is to be injected. The distributor comprises a central gas supply, a gas distribution box, a central support (3) for the box which can freely expand in any direction and a number of blow holes. The casing contains an opening for removing the granular material.

title: Drying installation for rendering waste products germ free, comprises a bio-heater in which product is subjected to heat generated by bacteriological action
abstract: Waste product is supplied to a bio-heater (1) formed by an enclosed space and then transported inside it whilst adding oxygen. Bacteriological action generates heat inside the space. An installation for drying waste products (especially animal and/or plant waste products) and rendering them germ-free comprises a bio-heater formed by an enclosed space. Waste product is supplied to this space and transported inside it whilst adding oxygen. Dwell time of the waste product in this space is sufficiently long for heat to be generated as a result of bacteriological action. An independent claim is also included for a method for drying waste products and rendering them germ-free using this installation, in which waste product from the bio-heater is supplied to a dryer (6), where it is dried using heat before being removed.

title: Hydro decanter slats separator.
abstract: ABSTRACTMETHOD AND SYSTEM FOR THE SEPARATION OFOILY COMPOUNDS AND SUSPENDED SOLIDS FROM RAINWATER The present invention is related to a lamella separator system for the separation of suspended solids and oily compounds from a wastewater stream, comprising:. A main inlet conduit (1) guiding the wastewater stream to the separator system. An inlet flow regulator (21) arranged to limit the influx to the separator system to a maximum influx,. A transit gutter (32) arranged for transferring the wastewater stream to a lamella separator,. A lamella separator arranged for receiving the wastewater and separating said wastewater into water, sediment and oily and/or volatile compounds, Characterised in that said transit gutter is arranged to provide a first flow of wastewater limited to a maximal first flow sufficient for cleaning said transit gutter from sediments by a first flow regulator (5), said first flow being gravitationally transported to a separator arranged for separating water, suspended solids and oily compounds, and wherein a main flow is provided by said transit gutter in case the influx exceeds the maximal first flow, wherein said main flow is fed laterally to said lamella separator by overflowing said transit gutter. (Figure 1)

title: Mounting pane of flame glazed glass, by cutting pane into pieces and not providing those pieces forming outer edges of pane with paint
abstract: The pane is cut into pieces and those forming the outer edges of the pane are arranged so that they are not provided with paint. A method for mounting a pane of glass printed and/or decorated with paint intended for flame glazing comprises cutting the pane into pieces so that all of the outer edges of the pane form a continuous arrangement of pieces which are not intended to be flame glazed. Independent claims are also included for the following: (a) applying paint intended for fire glazing onto glass, by sand-blasting or chemically matting the pane, the coarseness of the matt effect optionally determining the thickness of the paint coating; (b) bonding together two panes of glass with figures on both sides, so that the figures do not interfere with each other if fire glazing is carried out, by using opaque adhesive optionally containing a color or paint; (c) bonding together two panes of glass with figures on both sides, so that the figures do not interfere with each other if fire glazing is carried out, by bonding them together with an opaque film comprising any material and having any color; (d) enhancing the matt or sand-blasting effect of the background of a transparent glass pane without a mirror layer, by applying an opaque and optionally colored layer or paint to the rear side of a single pane or in between adjacent panes and/or on the rear side of multiple glazing; and (e) enhancing the matt or sand-blasting effect of the background of an optionally decorated transparent glass pane without a mirror layer, by applying an opaque and optionally colored film to the rear side of a single pane or in between adjacent panes and/or on the rear side of multiple glazing.

title: Immobilization of contaminants in sludge, by covering with finely divided material and injecting cement based grout
abstract: The sludge (11) is covered with a layer of finely divided material (12), at least some of the sludge is removed and a cement-based grout is injected into and mixed with the sludge. A method for immobilizing, deactivating and/or neutralizing contaminants present in sludge comprises covering a layer of sludge with a layer of finely divided material, removing at least some of the sludge layer, and injecting a cement-based grout into the sludge layer and mixing it with the latter. An Independent claim is also included for the process apparatus, comprising a rotor head rotatable about at least one axis and provided with one or more scoops (9) for removing the sludge, each scoop containing holes (21) for injecting the grout into the sludge at high pressure.

title: Removal of calcium, phosphate or heavy metals from water by precipitation comprises adding urea, a urea-hydrolyzing biocatalyst and a supplementary material
abstract: Removal of calcium, phosphate or heavy metals from water by precipitation comprises adding urea, adding a urea-hydrolyzing biocatalyst (enzyme or microbial, plant or animal material), and adding a supplementary material to complete the precipitation reaction. Independent claims are also included for: (1) forming precipitates under conditions determined by biocatalysis to produce residual products that are compatible with subsequent biological processes without pH adjustment; (2) additive comprising urea and a biocatalyst capable of converting the urea within minutes or hours to ammonia and carbonate for converting calcium salts to calcium carbonate, optionally in combination with other calcium salts; (3) additive comprising urea, a biocatalyst capable of converting the urea within minutes or hours to ammonia and carbonate, and calcium in reactive form for precipitating phosphate in the form of calcium phosphate and other salts.

title: Formula with dioxiranes for bleaching teeth
abstract: This invention relates to the discovery of a new type of bleaching formula with the active bleaching ingredient: dioxirane. Dioxiranes are organic compounds with a triple ring structure that contain one carbon and two oxygen atoms in the rings. Surprisingly, dioxiranes can bleach teeth (whitening). The required quantities of dioxirane are lower than the substance that is normally used (hydrogen peroxide), with a similar bleaching result. Dioxiranes are also more selective bleaching agents than hydrogen peroxide in spite of their lipophilic nature. The selectivity and the low dosage increase the safety of the bleaching procedure for patients. Dioxiranes can be used for both single and multiple phase bleaching procedures and can be combined with enzymatic bleaching steps and procedures whereby use is made of peroxides (e.g. hydrogen peroxide, urea peroxide...). The bleaching procedure with dioxiranes can be used in various application forms: bleaching agents, toothpaste powders, tooth gels, and mouthwashes, tooth polishing pastes.
