title: Formulations with tannase enzymes for whitening teeth
abstract: This invention relates to the discovery of a new whitening formula thatcontains the tannase enzyme. This enzyme appears to be capable of removingthe chemical tannin from teeth. Tannin is one of the categories of foodingredients that significantly contribute to teeth staining. The tannaseenzyme hydrolyses tannin quickly and efficiently, has an exceptionalselective function, and is therefore a safe whitening agent for teeth. It canbe applied in a single phase whitening process or be incorporated in amultiple phase process whereby the tannase treatment can be combined withtreatment using a generally recognized chemical oxidising whitening agent,such as hydrogen peroxide or urea peroxide, or with an oxidising enzymetreatment such as laccasse (with or without an accelerating mediator), alaccasse related enzyme, or another oxido reductase. The tannase enzyme canbe used in numerous application forms: whitening agents, toothpastes,powders, teeth gel, mouthwashes and dental abrasive powders.

title: Solid combustion element for disintegrating a combustion deposit LAYER AND METHOD FOR THE PRODUCTION OF SUCH AN ELEMENT.
abstract: This invention relates to a solid combustible element and a method for manufacturing it, whereby in the element an open or closed internal space is provided, and in this space is placed a chemical product for disintegrating a combustion deposit layer. When burning this element in a combustion device, such as e.g. a hearth or a stove, the product is released so that a combustion deposit layer (e.g. consisting of soot, ash and tar) formed on the walls of the combustion device or the smoke flue is disintegrated. The element is preferably formed by compressing an amount of loose particles of one or several solid combustible materials, such as among others wood waste and sawdust, without adding any binding agent. Such an element can be manufactured at lower raw material and production costs than the known elements in which such a product is incorporated. <IMAGE>

title: Method and products for whitening teeth
abstract: This invention relates to a new multi-step treatment procedure for whiteningteeth. Contrary to the current methods, the new method provides both animproved high bleaching power and a minimisation of side effects such asbrittle teeth. It is therefore more efficient and safer. We made thesurprising discovery that these advantages are attributable to the synergeticeffect between the whitening step using chemical bleaching agents (peroxidebased), and a pre-treatment using oxidising enzymes, carried out in thecontext of a multi-step whitening procedure. The efficiency of the multi-stepprocedure can be improved further by carrying out an alkali-cleansing stepsubsequent to the enzyme treatment and a cleansing treatment with a metal ioncomplexing product prior to the treatment with a peroxide whitening agent. Inan additional form of the invention, the oxido reductase enzyme treatment canbe improved through the addition of a perfluoro compound and an emulsifierfollowed by saturation of the enzyme solution with molecular oxygen.

title: Device for the transfer of energy and / or oplopsbare matter between a solid and a liquid.
abstract: The invention relates to a device for the transfer between a solid matter and a liquid, comprising an outer drum (1) and a perforated inner drum (2), means (4-5) for commonly rotating the drums (1 and 2), and means (18) situated inside the inner drum (2) for moving the solid matter (10). In between the two drums (1 and 2), a number of troughs (21) are formed which, during the rotation of the drums (1 and 2), bring up liquid (8) and pour it out higher up through the inner drum (2). Along the front rim of each trough (21), the drum (2) is provided with a drain opening (23), opposite to which means (24;26) are provided for preventing, when the drain opening (23), during the rotation of the drums (1-2), is at the bottom, the penetration of solid matter (10) through said opening.

title: Enamelled projection screen and method of manufacturing it.
abstract: The enamelled projection screen includes a substrate which is coated with at least one enamel layer which includes a pearlescent pigment and the gloss degree of which is between 1 and 40% on the 60° measuring head according to the ISO 2813 standard. This screen is manufactured by applying an enamel slurry on a substrate and burning it in at a high temperature, whereby, before the burning-in takes place, a quantity, preferably between 0.5 and 50 weight % in respect to the quantity of enamel in dry form, of a pearlescent pigment is added to the enamel slurry. The contents of pearlescent pigment are chosen sufficiently high and the temperature of burning-in sufficiently low in order to obtain the aforementioned gloss degree.

title: Moulding process
abstract: Straightforward moulding process, when carried out in a step by stepprocess, makes it possible to manufacture almost all 3-D shapes in onesingle moulding process and subject to follow up treatment the desiredcomplex shape can be obtained. The process can be fully automated, haspositive environmental aspects and creates the opportunity for the creationof new product types (e.g. a bottle with a variable wall thickness). One ormore fixed mould(s) made from soluble and/or compressible material are placein a mould or a matrix, after which the remaining voids are filled with therespective material. The filling can take place by means of casting,injection, rotation moulding, blow moulding or other application techniques.The following materials can be used as fillers, at the appropriate relativetemperatures: plastic, metal, glass, clay, polymers or other castingsubstances that can be injected. After ejection of the newly obtained wholeit is placed in a dissolving or compressing unit until the mould dissolves,liquefies or disintegrates into small fragments or molecules using forexample a high frequency field, ultrasonic vibrations, radiation, shockwaves or quenching. A moulded object remains. These finished objects can beextremely complex and useful.<IMAGE>

The filling can take place by means of casting,injection, rotation moulding, blow moulding or other application techniques.The following materials can be used as fillers, at the appropriate relativetemperatures: plastic, metal, glass, clay, polymers or other castingsubstances that can be injected. After ejection of the newly obtained wholeit is placed in a dissolving or compressing unit until the mould dissolves,liquefies or disintegrates into small fragments or molecules using forexample a high frequency field, ultrasonic vibrations, radiation, shockwaves or quenching. A moulded object remains. These finished objects can beextremely complex and useful.<IMAGE>

title: Method for regulating an aerobe water purification device and the waterpurification device regulated in accordance to this method
abstract: This invention relates to a method for regulating an aerobe waterpurification device, which is fitted with an aeration basin, which issupplied with an influent and from which effluent is led off, a separationunit over which the effluent from the aeration basin is transported, areturn pipe through which part of the silt from the separation unit isre-fed, and a discharge pipe which transports the remaining silt. Thespecific respiration rate of this unit is measured and compared to astandard reference respiration rate and the return silt discharge of thesilt from the separation unit to the aeration basin and/or the influentdischarge are modified accordingly with respect to the deviation factor ofthis measured specific respiration rate from the standard referencerespiration rate.<IMAGE>

title: A supplementary product for the removal of ammonia
abstract: This concerns a supplementary product, ABI, which is used for thebiological removal of ammonia under various conditions. ABI in its liquidform comprises a nitrifying microbial culture in suspension with therequired nutrients. With ABI in solid form the culture in suspension isadded to a mixture of absorbing and buffering substances such as peat, chalkand analogues. If there is ammonia present in the environment or it isproduced in the environment, it will be absorbed by the product andsubsequently transformed by the nitrifying agents present. The outcome beingthat ammonia concentration in the environment of the animals (mammals, birdsand fish), or humans is lower than would normally be expected in specificconditions. This reduction also creates a healthier environment, which mayreduce the occurrence of a number of diseases.

title: Decorative element, method for its manufacture, and decorative unit that contains such a decorative element
abstract: The invention relates to a decorative element (1) which has the shape and the appearance of a candle and/or with an outer surface that principally consists of candle wax, and which contains either a partially of fully incorporated holder (2) for one or more decorative objects (3) (such as flowers and/or plants). This holder (2) should preferably be made from a material (e.g. oasis, polystyrene foam, clay, silicone) in which holes for the decorative objects (3) can be made, and should preferably be fully enclosed in candle wax and integrated in the candle element (1), or is held by means of a fixing element (4) in an open incision (5) of the decorative element. Such a decorative element (1) can be readily incorporated in a decorative item (8) in which candles (9) and decorative elements (3) are incorporated.<IMAGE>

title: Device for the biological purification of contaminated water
abstract: Device for the biological purification of contaminated water, that comprises a bio-reactor (1) and at least one precipitator (2) with at least one block (13) of parallel plates, which are set up in the upper side of this reactor for the precipitation of the suspended particles, specific features being that a degassing device (4) is fitted prior to the entrance (5) of the precipitation unit (2) by which this is connected to the rest of the bio-reactor; below the precipitation unit (2) at least one collecting unit (3) for bio-gas has been attached of which at least one section borders the lower side of the precipitation unit (2) which on the one hand catches rising bio-gas and on the other hand forms a guide along which the precipitate is led to the lower end of the bio-reactor (1).<IMAGE>

title: Method for treatment of waste water
abstract: Method for the treatment of waste water by among other things nitrifying bacteria, in which the waste water and a slurry are mixed together in order to create a mixed liquid, in which the bacteria grow in the slurry, and into which slurry is injected, at least temporarily. Specific features are that at least once per day a maximum growth rate of the nitrifying bacteria or a variation thereof is estimated or determined, that the ammonium content of the treated water is measured, that a quantity per unit time of the slurry to be injected or a variation thereof is estimated or determined as a function of the determined maximum growth rate or a variation thereof and preferably as a function of the measured ammonium levels and of a desired ammonium level, and that the quantity per unit time of the slurry to be injected is altered as a function of the determined quantity per unit time or a variation thereof.<IMAGE>

title: Method and device for the removal of excess spray in a spraying cabin
abstract: Method for removing the excess paint spray in a spraying cabin, in particular for non water-based paints sprayed in a spraying cabin. An airflow is created with which this excess spray is guided over a water bath and this excess spray is removed from the water bath afterwards, specific features being that prior to contact with the water bath the excess spray is brought into contact with a water soluble absorption agent so that more than 60 per cent by weight, and preferably 80 per cent by weight of the excess spray particles do not form sticky granules in the water bath, after which the water bath is filtered in order to separate these granules from the water.<IMAGE>

title: An anaerobic flake-supporting agent for the improvement of the functioning of an anaerobic water purification system
abstract: This relates to a supplementary product that can improve the functioning of anaerobic reactors in water purification. The supplementary product induces the formation of good anaerobic granular slurry that is easier to hold in the reactor. The supplementary product comprises a broad spectrum of co-nutrients through which the growth of the slurry granules is advanced. In addition to this, the RACOD fraction and the Grandos Ylv-reducing capacity results in an improved and more robust granule, which increases the slurry stability. Grandos improves the conditions for better stability in the granular slurry, enabling it to be held in the reactor in a more efficient manner in spite of the production of bio-gas and the flow of the water to be purified through the reactor.

title: Method for the removal of impurities from metal objects by decomposition in a bath
abstract: Method for the removal of impurities from metal objects (1) that can be decomposed by specific purification agents such as a glycol when heated to working temperature, where the metal objects (1) to be purified are submerged in a bath (3) that contains such a purification agent and are kept in the bath at the said working temperature for a specific time period. Specific features are that the objects to be purified (1) are submerged in a bath (3) at atmospheric pressure with a purification agent of which the flash point is above 200 degrees and which does not reach its boiling point at atmospheric pressure and 300 degrees, with a small quantity of stabiliser, and that the working temperature of the bath (3) during the purification process is maintained between 180 and 300 degrees for the duration of the purification and that the object (1) is removed from the bath (3) at the said working temperature.<IMAGE>

title: Anti-wrinkle face tanner
abstract: With a composition of natural products without chemical additions such as: boiler basis cream, sesame oil, carrot oil, wheat germ oil, olive oil, almond oil, jojoba oil, ylang-ylang, anti-wrinkle cream. These aforementioned oils are ethereal oils. It is a yellow liquid cream. It is a completely new formula. It is specially composed in order to obtain a 5x quicker facial tanning with a little sun or a sun bed without wrinkle formation. The skin moisturisation is optimal due to the essential oils and ageing of the skin is reduced. Absorbs immediately in the skin and does not leave skin feeling greasy. Has not been tested on animals. Makes the skin feel silky smooth. Is extremely effective for application before sunning, after sunning before the sun bed and after the sun bed and also as an after-sun. Is very effective and has a pleasant scent.

title: Sun 5, quick tanning cream
abstract: Is a completely new formula, an INNOVATION in the world of sun cosmetics. Let yourself tan 5 times more quickly and safely with a little sun or a sun bed. Based on seaweed, which contains a lot of iodine, your skin will achieve a deep and fresh long lasting brown colour after only a few sessions of sunbathing. Contains natural essential oils that hydrate the skin optimally, reduce ageing and create a silky soft touch. Is an excellent REPLACEMENT for the traditional after-sun. Is immediately absorbed by the skin and leaves no fatty residue. Protected with natural protection factor. Is clearly a sensational solution, given the ozone layer problems that make it IMPERATIVE to avoid lengthy exposure to the sun. Has not been tested on animals.

title: Metal fiber AND METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING THEREOF.
abstract: PCT No. PCT/EP98/00158 Sec. 371 Date Nov. 5, 1998 Sec. 102(e) Date Nov. 5, 1998 PCT Filed Jan. 12, 1998 PCT Pub. No. WO98/31491 PCT Pub. Date Jul. 23, 1998The invention relates to a metal fiber agglomerate comprising fibers with an equivalent diameter of between 1 mu m and 30 mu m and with a length of between 1 mm and 20 mm, with the feature that the core of the agglomerate contains at least one bundle of fibers oriented substantially parallel to one another, while the casing consists of a network of randomly oriented fibers. The invention also relates to a process for the manufacture of these metal fiber agglomerates, characterized by a succession of steps in which the agglomerate is subjected to a continuous tumbling movement.

title: Film provided with a transfer varnish coating
abstract: Porous film that principally comprises mainly linear ultra-high molecular polyethylene with an intrinsic viscosity of at least 18 cm3/g, whereby the said film is porous and has a pore volume of at least 35 volume percent in relation to the total volume of the film, and the film contains 50-90 weight percent in fine particles of a silicon compound that cannot be dissolved in water, which is distributed across the film. Whereby a transfer varnish coating is applied to the film that has the following components: mineral oils 5-20 weight percent preferably 7-12 weight percent; vegetable oils 25-45 weight percent, preferably 30-40 weight percent; modified colophonium resin 20-40 weight percent, preferably 25-35 weight percent, filler 10-30 weight percent, preferably 15-25 weight percent, desiccation substance 0-, 5.5 weight percent, preferably 1-3 weight percent. Label obtained from such a film and bottle crates provided with such a label.

title: Method for manufacturing a surface structure and tools equipped with such a surface structure
abstract: Method for realising a surface-structure, whereby the crater-shaped surface elements (3) are created, whereby the whole is characterised by three main areas, namely a first main area (W1) that is defined by the tops of the piles of material (5-9) of the material from the craters, and a second area (W2) that is defined by the base of the craters and a third main area (W3) that is defined by the surface of the material in which the surface structure (2) should be realised, characterised by the fact that the presence of the said third main area (W3) is pressed down by an objective choice of the surface geometry, more specifically through an appropriate choice of the mutual situation of the crater-shaped surface elements (3).<IMAGE>

title: A supplementary product for the improvement of the action of active slurry systems in the water purification
abstract: This concerns a supplementary product that can improve and optimise the general action of active slurry systems under various conditions and circumstances. The supplementary product improves the precipitation by generating radicals with the outcome being an improved flake formation and an increase to the ash levels of the slurry flakes. Through the improved flake formation and higher density the precipitation is improved. Particularly in unfavourable conditions, such as low temperatures and high slurry loads, the supplementary product has a positive effect on the efficiency of the purification process. The supplementary product comprises a broad spectrum of co-nutrients. Slurry flakes with a more diverse microbial composition are achieved through this, with a higher quantity of higher organisms such as ciliates and rotifers, through which the efficiency of the purification capacity in the active slurry system is improved.

title: Purification unit
abstract: For the purification of liquids such as waste water, comprising a number of reservoirs, whereby each reservoir performs a specific step in the purification process. The reservoirs are formed by a round, for example cylindrical or elliptical external wall, which encloses a compartment that is divided by one or more internal walls. The internal walls and the external walls are fitted at various distances around a common axis. One of the reservoirs is an anaerobic reservoir for anaerobic purification, whereas an anoxic reservoir, in which an anoxic environment prevails, serves the purpose of removing nitrates. A thickening reservoir serves the purpose of thickening phosphate rich slurry that is transported to the thickening reservoir from the anaerobic reservoir. Between the anaerobic reservoir and the anoxic reservoir, there is a mixing reservoir for mixing the slurry from the anaerobic reservoir with the return slurry from a precipitation tank. Partitions have been placed in the top of the anaerobe reservoir.<IMAGE>

title: Method for producing, packaging and use of regulated gist room.
abstract: Process for shipping and utilisation of a cream of yeast after prolonged periods of undisturbed storage comprises: (i) stabilising the cream of yeast with a food additive chosen from edible thickeners and stabilisers; (ii) charging the stabilised cream of yeast, cooled to below 10 degrees C, to a fully or quasi sterile container having a volume of about 100-3000 (preferably 200-3000) l; (iii) placing the container during transport, storage and drawing off of the cream of yeast for use, in an enclosure maintained at a temperature of -1 to 10 (preferably 0-5) degrees C ; (iv) withdrawing the cream of yeast, by gravity and/or by metering pump, from the vicinity of the bottom of the container and feeding it into one or more distribution pipes which feed the stabilised cream of yeast into ≥ 1 kneaders, and (v) cleaning the distributor pipe(s) in situ with a cleaning solution after a predetermined period of operation. The container into which the stabilised cream of yeast is charged may be flexible and disposable, or rigid and re-useable. The cream of yeast may be maintained in circulation round a loop running from the base of the container, through a circulation pump, round a heat- insulated recirculation loop which serves the kneaders, and back via a cooling coil to cool the cream of yeast to 5 degrees C. The cream of yeast is returned to the container via a tube extending down to near the base of the container to maintain the contents of the container in a mixed state.

title: RUBBER MODIFIED POLYAMIDE polymer composition.
abstract: The invention relates to a polymer composition comprising 5 to 79 wt.% of a graft copolymer of vinyl aromatic monomers and monomers chosen from the group of C1-C4 alkyl methacrylates, C1-C4 alkyl acrylates, methacrylonitrile and acrylonitrile on a rubber, 90 to 20 wt.% of a thermoplastic polyamide polymer and 1 to 50 wt.% of a polymer comprising a vinyl aromatic monomer, at least one monomer chosen from the group of C1-C4 alkyl methacrylates, C1-C4 alkyl acrylates, methacrylonitrile and acrylonitrile, and 0.2 to 30 mol% of a compound containing alpha , beta -unsaturated dicarboxylic anhydride, the wt.% of the components being relative to the total weight of the components, characterized in that the composition also comprises at least one monofunctional dicarboxylic-anhydride-containing compound (D) with a weight average molecular weight of less than 1500 g/mol. The polymer composition exhibits excellent mechanical properties, in particular a high impact resistance.

title: Mixture for use in a film-forming covering and method to achieve such a mixture
abstract: Mixture for use in film-forming coverings, whereby this mixture is a composition of filling agents or pigments and organic micro-spheres that comprise a thermoplastic shell which encloses a gas forming expansion medium. Specific features are that the composite, with respect to the total weight comprises 99 to 50 per cent by weight of pigments or filling agents with an average diameter less than 20 micrometers and a density greater than 1g/cm3 on the one hand, and 1 to 50 per cent by weight of organic micro-spheres with a diameter smaller than 50 micrometers and a density lower than 0.1 g/cm3 on the other hand. This mixture is homogenous and mechanically stable, leaves no sieve residue on a 100-micrometre sieve and is finer than 100 micrometers according to ASTM D1210.

title: Star shaped branched polyamide
abstract: The invention relates to a star shaped branched polyamide that has a high thermal stability and can be produced in existing production equipment for the corresponding linear polyamide. This is a achieved by creating at least 3 polyamide branches from a multifunctional core with functional groups chosen from the set of amino or carboxyl groups, in which the amino or carboxyl groups are present on an acyclic compound that (if it is branched) has its branches at a carbon atom, or via an acyclic chain to a heterocyclic compound that has point symmetry. The method for the preparation is executed under the conditions for the preparation of the corresponding linear polyamide. It is apparently possible to obtain a product after a short polymerisation time with mechanical properties that are comparable to those of the linear polyamide after a significantly longer polymerisation time. Examples of suitable multifunctional cores are 2,4,6-tris-aminocapric acid-1,3,5-triazine and 4-aminomethyl-1,1-octanediamine.

title: Procedure and device for biological water purification and slurry processing
abstract: A procedure for biological water purification and slurry processing, in which the water purification is done using bacteria, where purified water and the slurry present in the water are separated by means of a centrifuge device (2) or a cyclone, allowing the slurry to be mixed with the active bacteria. The slurry is either added to the as yet unpurified water (5) or treated by means of a centrifuge treatment (2) or a cyclone, allowing slurry to be obtained that is suitable for discharge. A device for the application of this procedure, comprising a device (1) for the purification of water by means of bacteria, and both for the separation of the purified water and the slurry and for the treatment of the slurry in a centrifuge device (2) or a cyclone. The separation of the water and slurry, and the treatment of the slurry should preferably be done using the same centrifuge device (2) or cyclone. The device can be operated with only one basin (1).<IMAGE>

title: Device for combating algae and bacteria through ion exchange of copper and silver ions in the swimming pool sector of which the anodes are inbuilt in a pipeline
abstract: By making use of flat plates with a thickness of 4mm for the anodes, and by mounting these in a unique manner in the horizontal direction of a traditional 50 mm pipeline, parallel to the water flow an optimal ion exchange and distribution is achieved. In order to increase the effectiveness of the invention and to maximise the specific properties of both the copper and the silver, use is made of a separate control system with polarity inversion for both the two copper anodes and the two silver anodes that are mounted between the filter pump and the filter and the swimming pool's return feed pipeline respectively.

title: PROCESS FOR THE PREPARATION OF A N-alkoxy- (tetra- or hexahydro) -phthalimide AND PROCESS FOR THE PREPARATION OF A N-alkoxy amine.
abstract: Method for the preparation of N-alkoxy-(tetra-or hexahydro)-phthalimide according to formula (I), R1 being methyl or ethyl and R2 and R3 being each independently of one another hydrogen or alkyl with 1-4C atoms of R2 and R3 together with the carbon atom to which they are bonded forming a ring with 5 or 6 C atoms, wherein the salt of the corresponding N-hydroxy-(tetrahydro- or hexahydro)-phthalimide according to formula II; with R2 and R3 as defined and M being an alkali metal, is brought into contact with R1Cl as alkylating agent, wherein R1 has the mentioned meaning. The N-alkoxy-(tetra- or hexahydro)-phthalimide can afterwards be converted with the aid of hydroxylamine sulphate into the corresponding alkoxyamine after which the alkoxyamine obtained can be isolated. In this manner a simple and commercially attractive process for the preparation of O-(m)ethylhydroxylamine can be implemented.

title: COMPOSITION FOR PROTECTING WATER REPELLENT AND MAKING A siliceous substrate.
abstract: PCT No. PCT/EP95/03785 Sec. 371 Date May 7, 1997 Sec. 102(e) Date May 7, 1997 PCT Filed Sep. 25, 1995 PCT Pub. No. WO96/09993 PCT Pub. Date Apr. 4, 1996A mixture is proposed for treating substrates, in particular glass or ceramic material, containing silicon compounds. The proposed mixture consists of the following components: (a) component A, which is formed from at least the polysiloxane with Si-H bonds; (b) component B, which is formed from at least one bi-, tri- or tetrafunctional silane, an alkoxy or CH3COO rest acting as the functional group; and (c) component C, which is formed from at least one strong, anhydrous organic or inorganic acid. The mixture can be applied to the substrate to particular advantage after being diluted with a solvent.

title: Method and apparatus for processing waste with power kalorisch.
abstract: PCT No. PCT/BE95/00058 Sec. 371 Date Dec. 20, 1996 Sec. 102(e) Date Dec. 20, 1996 PCT Filed Jun. 20, 1995 PCT Pub. No. WO95/35352 PCT Pub. Date Dec. 28, 1995A thermal method for continuously processing waste having a calorific value and any degree of moisture, wherein waste is put in a flow of hot, heat-resistant, heat-exchanging material which is warmer than 100 DEG C. The heat exchanging material cools due to heat exchange, the waste dries and the non-evaporated waste components are heated. The cooled, heat-exchanging material is subsequently separated from the dried waste materials and the separated dried waste material is mixed with a percentage of the separated heat-exchanging material. The dried waste material and heat-exchanging material mixture is subsequently heated to pyrolyze waste material and heat the heat exchanging material in preparation for its subsequently use in the continuous process.

title: Method for the immobilization of particles suspended in water
abstract: A method for the immobilization of particles suspended in water, whichcontains industrial waste that is contaminated with hazardous organic andinorganic matter. By treating the waste material with a fixing agent, thesaid fixing agent must contain organophillic clay, an oxidation agent andiron and permanganate sulphate, and if required a hardening agent, inaddition to the fixing agent a compounding agent is added. The preferableform of compounding agent is either granulated or ground steel slag;granulated or ground blast furnace slag, or a combination thereof. Accordingto a distinctive execution method of the method in question a granulatingagent is also added. Sieved sand is particularly suitable. Fly ash or acombination of fly ash and sieved sand is a suitable alternative. A compoundcontaining calcium is correspondingly added in order to create the enclosureof the compounds adsorbed in the clay matrix into this matrix, e.g. calciumhydroxide or a substance that contains calcium hydroxide.

title: Method for processing a black-white silver halogen photographic materialwith spectrally sensitised emulsions
abstract: A description of the processing method of a silver halogen photographicmaterial comprising a carrier, this carrier has at least one side which hasa layer containing cubic silver halogen crystals. The said crystals arespectrally sensitised with a compound with the general formula (I), where Lrepresents hydrogen, a substituted or non-substituted alkyl group, Z1 and Z2both independently represent a sulphur or a selenium atom, X and M+respectively represent a negative and a positive ion, used to neutralize theelectrical charge of the molecule, p and Q both independently have a numericvalue which is equal to 0 or 1, whereby, p=1 and q=0 if the molecule ispositively charged, p=0 and q=1 if the molecule is negatively charged, andp== and q=0 if the molecule is neutral; R5 and R6 both independentlyrepresent a substituted or non-substituted aryl group or a substituted ornon-substituted alkyl group, whereby at least one of the R5 and R6 isrepresented by formula (II), (III) or (IV)...<IMAGE>
